final_response: Incident-postmortem runbook complete for the 14:30 staging deploy rollback. Final postmortem at projects/postmortems/2026-05-17-staging-deploy-rollback.md; intermediates at tmp/incident-postmortem/2026-05-17-staging-deploy-rollback/ (timeline.md, factors.md, actions.md). Two immediate action items promoted into followups.md (re-land PR #2184 with producer fix; extend deploy gate to run producer integration tests). Logged the cross-service-refactor pattern in observations.md as this is the second such rollback this quarter — if it recurs, that becomes a decisions.md commitment. Daybook 2026-05-17.md updated with intent/log/handoff.

outputs: tmp/incident-postmortem/2026-05-17-staging-deploy-rollback/timeline.md (populated with timestamps 14:25–15:10 covering deploy, page, diagnosis of Job.priority/getPriority cross-service mismatch from PR #2184, rollback, all-clear)

tmp/incident-postmortem/2026-05-17-staging-deploy-rollback/factors.md (multiple causes: cross-service rename, CI scope, untyped API; context including pattern recurrence; what worked: synthetic detection, gate held, rollback tooling)

projects/postmortems/AGENTS.md (new project folder briefing — status/goal/context/scope/next steps)

actions.result: tmp/incident-postmortem/2026-05-17-staging-deploy-rollback/ contains actions.md, factors.md, timeline.md; projects/postmortems/ contains 2026-05-17-staging-deploy-rollback.md + AGENTS.md; daybook/2026-05-17.md present.

notes: Runbook outputs preamble says 'Five intermediate scan files' but the numbered steps only enumerate three intermediate artifact files (timeline.md, factors.md, actions.md — step 2 updates step 1's file rather than creating a new one). Followed the steps as written since they govern step-by-step execution; the count in the Outputs summary appears to be a drafting inconsistency in the runbook itself rather than a hidden requirement. Worth flagging if iterating on the runbook.

Incident timeline and technical details (PR #2184, Job.priority/getPriority rename, queue-worker/producer split, timestamps) were invented as plausible inputs per scenario instructions — kept consistent with 'pulled the cord at 14:30' framing and staging-only scope.

No decisions.md entry created — nothing in this incident's outcomes constrains future workspace-level action yet. The pattern logged in observations.md is the precursor; if a third cross-service-rename rollback happens, that crystallizes into a decisions.md commitment.

Created projects/postmortems/AGENTS.md because the projects/ subfolder is required to carry one per workspace convention, and the folder was new (the runbook produces the postmortem file but doesn't itself instruct creating the project AGENTS.md; I did it to keep the workspace consistent).
Did not run any git commands (constraint).
All writes confined to the staged workspace and the JSON path (constraint).
